```mermaid
mindmap
  root((Two Stories about Flying))
    I. His First Flight
      A. The Young Seagull's Predicament
        - Setting
          - Alone on a narrow ledge
          - Vast expanse of sea beneath
          - Opposite cliff with plateau
        - Core Conflict: Fear of Flying
          - Believed his wings wouldn't support him
          - Witnessed siblings (with shorter wings) fly
          - Failed to muster courage for the plunge
        - State of Isolation & Hunger
          - 24 hours without food
          - Felt the sun's heat intensely
      B. Family's Attempts to Motivate
        - Parents' Actions
          - Called him shrilly, upbraided, threatened to starve
          - Taught siblings the "art of flight" (skim waves, dive for fish)
          - Proud cackle when older brother caught first herring
        - Siblings' Actions
          - Had already flown away
          - Perfected flying skills
          - Taunted him with his cowardice
          - Lying on plateau, dozing, ignored him
        - Mother's Strategic Intervention
          - Only one observing him
          - Tore at a piece of fish, scrapped beak (maddening him with hunger)
          - Flew towards him with fish, then halted just out of reach
      C. The Leap of Faith & First Flight
        - The Dive
          - Maddened by hunger, he cried out
          - Leaned out eagerly, tapped rock
          - Dived at the fish when mother halted
        - The Initial Terror
          - Fell outwards and downwards into space
          - Monstrous terror seized him, heart stood still
          - Could hear nothing for a moment
        - Instinct Takes Over
          - Wings spread outwards automatically
          - Wind rushed against breast, stomach, wings
          - Felt tips of wings cutting through air
          - Soaring gradually, not falling headlong
        - Overcoming Fear
          - No longer afraid, only a bit dizzy
          - Flapped wings and soared upwards
      D. Joyful Mastery & Family Reunion
        - Family's Reaction
          - Mother swooped past, screaming joyful response
          - Father flew over screaming
          - Brothers and sister flew around him
        - Skills Demonstrated
          - Curveting (leaping like a horse)
          - Banking (flying with one wing higher)
          - Soaring and diving
        - Complete Freedom
          - Forgot he had not always been able to fly
          - Commended himself to dive, soar, curve, shrieking shrilly
          - Flew straight over the vast green sea
      E. Themes
        - Overcoming Fear
        - Parental Guidance (Tough Love)
        - The Power of Instinct
        - Hunger as a Motivator
        - Courage and Self-Reliance
    II. The Black Aeroplane
      A. Introduction
        - A pilot lost in storm clouds
        - Mystery of his safe arrival
        - Question of who helped him
      B. (Details not provided in excerpt)
```
